attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Move the pen to the dresser.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

1. Objects and Their Types:
   - The scene contains a dark brown wooden dresser, which is a receptacle (as it has a flat top surface that can hold objects and is used as a storage surface in the context of the task).
   - A single pen is visible on the top surface of the dresser. The pen is an object (not a receptacle).
   - A hexagonal-shaped container (possibly a small bin or pot) is on the floor next to the dresser. It is not mentioned in the domain types and is not relevant to the instruction.
   - A white cylindrical object (possibly a cup or vase) with three brown stick-like protrusions (perhaps chopsticks or decorative sticks) is on top of the dresser. This object is not relevant to the instruction and is not defined in the domain.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 dresser (receptacle)
   - 1 pen (object)
   - 1 hexagonal container (irrelevant object)
   - 1 white cylindrical object with sticks (irrelevant object)

3. Spatial Relationships:
   - The pen is currently located on the top surface of the dresser. This means the pen is “in” or “on” the dresser receptacle (inReceptacle pen dresser is true).
   - The hexagonal container is on the wooden floor, adjacent to the dresser, but not interacting with any domain-relevant objects.
   - The white cylindrical object with sticks is also on the dresser, sharing the same surface as the pen.

4. Current State of Objects Relevant to the Planning Task:
   - The pen is stationary and not held by any agent.
   - The dresser is not openable (it is a surface, not a cabinet or drawer), so openable(dresser) is false.
   - The pen is not clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled — none of those predicates apply.
   - The agent is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

5. Visual Properties:
   - The dresser is dark brown with a wood grain texture and has a rectangular top surface.
   - The pen is small, cylindrical, and appears to be blue or dark gray with a metallic tip. It is lying horizontally on the dresser.
   - The hexagonal container is dark blue or gray with a purple interior and is positioned on the floor to the right of the dresser.
   - The white cylindrical object is matte white with three thin, brown, vertical sticks protruding from its top.
   - The floor is light brown wood laminate with a glossy reflection visible near the dresser.
   - The wall to the right is a textured beige or cream color.

Note: The instruction “Move the pen to the dresser” is already satisfied, as the pen is already on the dresser. No action is required to fulfill the instruction based on the current scene.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem move_pen_to_dresser)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        dresser - receptacle
        pen - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 dresser)  ; Agent is initially at the dresser location as per scene context
        (inReceptacle pen dresser)   ; Pen is already on the dresser
        (not (openable dresser))     ; Dresser is not openable (it's a surface)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))      ; Agent is not holding any object
    )
    (:goal (and (inReceptacle pen dresser)))
)
```
attempts.problem:

    )
    (:init
        (atLocation agent1 dresser)  ; Agent is initially at the dresser location as per scene context
        (inReceptacle pen dresser)   ; Pen is already on the dresser
        (not (openable dresser))     ; Dresser is not openable (it's a surface)
        (not (holdsAny agent1))      ; Agent is not holding any object
    )
    (:goal (and (inReceptacle pen dresser)))
)

On the floor, to the right of the dresser and adjacent to the wall, sits a hexagonal-shaped container or bin with a dark blue rim and a purple interior. Inside this container, there is a single thin, light-colored object resembling a pen or pencil, oriented diagonally within the bin.

The robot agent is not currently at any object’s location. The pen is not being held by the agent, and no object is currently in the dresser. The pen is not clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled — none of those predicates apply.

The goal is to move the pen to the dresser. To achieve this, the agent must first go to the location of the hexagonal container (to access the pen), pick up the pen, then go to the dresser, and put the pen on it.

No knives, microwaves, fridges, or sinks are visible in the scene. The only relevant objects for the task are the pen (object), the hexagonal container (object, holding the pen), and the dresser (receptacle).

The pen is visually distinguishable by its thin, elongated shape and light color, contrasting with the dark interior of the hexagonal container. The dresser is large, rectangular, and dark brown, occupying the upper portion of the image. The hexagonal container is smaller, placed on the floor, and has a distinct geometric shape and color contrast.

No objects are currently in the dresser, and no objects are being held by the agent. The pen is currently inside the hexagonal container, which is on the floor near the wall. The dresser is empty and ready to receive the pen.

```pddl
(define (problem move_pen_to_dresser)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        pen - object
        hexagonal_container - object
        dresser - receptacle
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation robot pen))
        (not (atLocation robot hexagonal_container))
        (not (atLocation robot dresser))
        (inReceptacle pen hexagonal_container)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (holds robot pen))
        (not (isClean pen))
        (not (isHot pen))
        (not (isCool pen))
        (not (isSliced pen))
        (not (isToggled pen))
        (not (opened dresser))
        (not (openable dresser)) ; dresser is not openable as per scene description
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle pen dresser)
            (not (inReceptacle pen hexagonal_container))
        )
    )
)
```

        hexagonal_container - object
        dresser - receptacle
    )

        (not (atLocation robot pen))
        (not (atLocation robot hexagonal_container))
        (not (atLocation robot dresser))
        (inReceptacle pen hexagonal_container)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (holds robot pen))
        (not (isClean pen))
        (not (isHot pen))
        (not (isCool pen))
        (not (isSliced pen))
        (not (isToggled pen))
        (not (opened dresser))
        (not (openable dresser)) ; dresser is not openable as per scene description
    )
    (:goal
        (and
            (inReceptacle pen dresser)
            (not (inReceptacle pen hexagonal_container))
        )
    )
)

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
